title: Salient visual relevancy of feature assessments by machine learning models
abstract: Various embodiments of the present disclosure are directed to a salient medical imaging controller (80) employing an artificial intelligence engine (40) and a medical image display engine (50). In operation, the artificial intelligence engine (40) includes one or more machine learning models (42) trained to render a feature assessment of a volumetric medical image. The medical image display engine (50) generates a volumetric salient image illustrative of the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image, and reslices a salient image from the volumetric salient image based on a relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image by the machine learning model(s) (42).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A salient medical imaging controller (80), comprising: a memory (85) storing an artificial intelligence engine (40) and a medical image display engine (50), wherein the artificial intelligence engine (40) includes at least one machine learning model (42); and at least one processor (81) in communication with the memory (85), the at least one processor (81) configured to: apply the at least one machine learning model (42) to medical image data representative of at least one feature of a volumetric medical image to render a feature assessment of the volumetric medical image; generate, via the medical image display engine (50), a volumetric salient image illustrative of the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image by the at least one machine learning model (42); and reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), a planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on a relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image by the at least one machine learning model (42). 2. The salient medical imaging controller (80) of claim 1 , wherein the reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image includes the at least one processor (81) further configured to: identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image including at least one salient voxel. 3. The salient medical imaging controller (80) of claim 1 , wherein the reslice, via the 

 medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image includes the at least one processor (81) further configured to: identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image, wherein, among a plurality of salient planes intersecting the volumetric salient image, the resliced salient plane includes one of a highest quantity, a highest spatial distribution, a highest summation or a highest average of the salient voxels. 4. The salient medical imaging controller (80) of claim 1 , wherein the reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image includes the at least one processor (81) further configured to: identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image including a centroid of the salient voxels. 5. The salient medical imaging controller (80) of claim 1 , wherein the reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image includes the at least one processor (81) further configured to: identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image parallel to a coordinate plane of the volumetric salient image and including at least one salient voxel. 6. The salient medical imaging controller (80) of claim 1 , wherein the reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image includes the at least one processor (81) further configured to: identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; receive a user specification of a center point of an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image; and derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from the user-specified center point of the intersection of the resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image relative to the plurality of salient voxels. 7. The salient medical imaging controller (80) of claim 1 , wherein the reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image includes the at least one processor (81) further configured to: identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; receive a user specification of an orientation of an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image; and derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from the user-specified orientation of the intersection of the resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image relative to the plurality of salient voxels. 8. The salient medical imaging controller (80) of claim 1 , wherein the planar salient image is one of a heatmap, a feature map or a planar activation image. 9. A non-transitory machine-readable storage medium encoded with instructions for execution by at least one processor (81), the non-transitory machine-readable storage 

 medium comprising instructions to: apply an artificial intelligence engine (40) including at least one machine learning model (42) to medical image data representative of at least one feature of a volumetric medical image to render a feature assessment of the volumetric medical image; generate, via a medical image display engine (50), a volumetric salient image illustrative of the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image by the at least one machine learning model (42); and reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), a salient image from the volumetric salient image based on a relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image. 10. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 9, wherein the instructions to reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image include: instructions to identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and instructions to derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image including at least one salient voxel. 11. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 9, wherein the instructions to reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image include: instructions to identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and instructions to derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image, wherein, among a plurality of salient planes intersecting the volumetric salient image, the resliced salient plane includes one of a highest quantity, a highest 

 spatial distribution, a highest summation or a highest average of the salient voxels. 12. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 9, wherein the instructions to reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image include: instructions to identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and instructions to derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image including a centroid of the salient voxels. 13. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 9, wherein the instructions to reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image include: instructions to identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and instructions to derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image parallel to a coordinate plane of the volumetric salient image and including at least one salient voxel. 14. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 9, wherein the instructions to reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image include: instructions to identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; instructions to receive a user specification of a center point of an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image; and instructions to derive a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image 

 within the volumetric salient image from the user-specified center point of the intersection of the resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image relative to the plurality of salient voxels. 15. The non-transitory machine -readable storage medium of claim 9, wherein the instructions to reslice, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image include: instructions to identify a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; instructions to receive a user specification of an orientation of an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image; and instructions to derive an center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from the user-specified orientation of the intersection of the resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image relative to the plurality of salient voxels. 16. A salient medical imaging method, comprising: applying an artificial intelligence engine (40) including at least one machine learning model (42) to medical image data representative of at least one feature of a volumetric medical image to render a feature assessment of the volumetric medical image; generating, via a medical image display engine (50), a volumetric salient image illustrative of the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image by the at least one machine learning model (42); and reslicing, via the medical image display engine (50), a salient image from the volumetric salient image based on a relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image by the at least one machine learning model (42). 17. The salient medical imaging method of claim 16, wherein the reslicing, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric 

 medical image includes: identifying, via the medical image display engine (50), a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and deriving, via the medical image display engine (50), a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image including at least one salient voxel. 18. The salient medical imaging method of claim 16, wherein the reslicing, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image includes: identifying, via the medical image display engine (50), a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and deriving, via the medical image display engine (50), a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image, wherein, among a plurality of salient planes intersecting the volumetric salient image, the resliced salient plane includes one of a highest quantity, a highest spatial distribution, a highest summation or a highest average of the salient voxels. 19. The salient medical imaging method of claim 16, wherein the reslicing, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image includes: identifying, via the medical image display engine (50), a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; and deriving, via the medical image display engine (50), a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from one of: 

 an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image including a centroid of the salient voxels, and an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image parallel to a coordinate plane of the volumetric salient image and including at least one salient voxel. 20. The salient medical imaging method of claim 16, wherein the reslicing, via the medical image display engine (50), of the planar salient image from the volumetric salient image based on the relevancy of each feature to the feature assessment of the volumetric medical image includes: identifying, via the medical image display engine (50), a plurality of salient voxels within the volumetric salient image, each salient voxel having a relevancy level exceeding a relevancy threshold; receiving, via the medical image display engine (50), at least one of a user specification of a center point and an orientation of an intersection of a resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image; and deriving, via the medical image display engine (50), a center point and an orientation of the planar salient image within the volumetric salient image from the at least one of the user-specified center point and the user-specified orientation of the intersection of the resliced salient plane with the volumetric salient image relative to the plurality of salient voxels.
